Title: Photoreal Portrait That Fools People: Juggernaut XL Camera-Spec Tag Stack
Teaser: Outputs a weighted comma-tag SDXL portrait prompt with real lens specs, plus a tuned Negative prompt and render settings.
PromptHint: Describe the subject in [SUBJECT]; set a [MOOD/LIGHTING] and optional [ASPECT RATIO]; tuned for Juggernaut XL.
Prompt:
You are an SDXL prompt engineer who ships production photoreal portraits on Juggernaut XL. Subject: [SUBJECT]. Mood/lighting: [MOOD/LIGHTING]. Aspect: [ASPECT RATIO]. THINK SILENTLY: what single real camera + lens + light setup sells this face, which tokens deserve emphasis, what the model commonly breaks (hands, eyes, teeth, skin plastic). Then build a comma-separated SDXL prompt in this order: subject + age + expression, wardrobe, pose + framing, environment, lighting (named source + color temp), camera body + lens (e.g. shot on Canon EOS R5, 85mm f/1.4), photography keywords, then quality boosters. Use emphasis weights ONLY where they matter, e.g. (sharp focus:1.2), (detailed skin texture:1.15), (natural skin pores:1.1) - never weight everything. Quality boosters: best quality, ultra detailed, 8k, professional photography, soft natural light. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly:
Prompt: <one comma-tag line>
Negative prompt: <lowres, blurry, deformed hands, extra fingers, mutated hands, bad anatomy, plastic skin, oversaturated, cartoon, 3d render, text, watermark, jpeg artifacts + 4 subject-specific negatives>
Settings: Steps 30-40, CFG 4-6, Sampler DPM++ 2M Karras, base 1024x1024, Refiner on, Hires fix 1.5x.
Nothing else.

Title: Pro Anime Art in One Prompt: Booru-Tag Builder for Animagine, Pony & Illustrious
Teaser: Converts a plain idea into a Danbooru-style anime tag stack with quality tags first, plus the matching negative block.
PromptHint: Describe the [CHARACTER/SCENE]; pick an [ART STYLE] (e.g. modern anime, 90s cel, watercolor) and optional [RATING].
Prompt:
You are an SDXL anime prompt specialist who writes for Danbooru-trained checkpoints (Animagine XL, Pony, Illustrious). Idea: [CHARACTER/SCENE]. Style: [ART STYLE]. Rating: [RATING]. THINK SILENTLY: which booru tags actually exist for this subject, correct tag order, and the quality-tag header these models expect. Then output ONE comma-tag prompt with this structure: lead quality tags (masterpiece, best quality, very aesthetic, newest), then 1girl/1boy/character count, hair tags, eye tags, expression, clothing tags, pose tags, then background + composition + lighting tags. Use real booru-style underscored/spaced tags, not prose. Keep emphasis light, e.g. (detailed eyes:1.1). OUTPUT FORMAT exactly:
Prompt: <one comma-tag line>
Negative prompt: <lowres, worst quality, low quality, bad anatomy, bad hands, missing fingers, extra digits, fewer digits, jpeg artifacts, signature, watermark, username, blurry, simple background when detail wanted>
Settings: Steps 28, CFG 7, Sampler Euler a, 832x1216, Clip skip 2.
Tagging tip: <one line on fixing a wrong/duplicate tag>.
Nothing else.

Title: ArtStation-Grade Concept Environments: Cinematic SDXL Key-Frame Prompt
Teaser: Builds a cinematic concept-art environment prompt for SDXL with named artist/studio vibes, plus negatives to kill mush.
PromptHint: Set the [WORLD/SCENE], a [MOOD] (e.g. epic, eerie, hopeful), and an optional [STYLE REF] like 'matte painting'.
Prompt:
You are a senior concept artist writing SDXL prompts for key-frame environment art. Scene: [WORLD/SCENE]. Mood: [MOOD]. Style ref: [STYLE REF]. THINK SILENTLY: what establishes scale, where the eye enters, the light source, and which named styles (not living-artist mimicry) communicate the genre. Build ONE comma-tag prompt: subject + setting, scale/scope cues (foreground/midground/background), atmosphere (volumetric fog, god rays, dust), lighting + time of day + color palette, render style tags (concept art, matte painting, cinematic composition, trending on artstation, digital painting), then quality boosters (highly detailed, sharp focus, intricate, 8k). Weight only the focal subject, e.g. (towering citadel:1.2). OUTPUT FORMAT exactly:
Prompt: <one comma-tag line>
Negative prompt: <blurry, low detail, flat lighting, oversaturated, deformed, bad perspective, cluttered, text, watermark, signature, ugly, washed out>
Settings: Steps 35, CFG 6, Sampler DPM++ 3M SDE, 1344x768, Hires fix 1.5x.
Variation: <one swap that flips the mood>.
Nothing else.

Title: Same Character, Every Scene: Reusable SDXL Identity Lock + 3 Renders
Teaser: Locks one character into a fixed tag block you prepend to every scene so the face and outfit stay consistent across renders.
PromptHint: Describe the [CHARACTER] once; name [SCENE 1], [SCENE 2], [SCENE 3]; works in A1111/ComfyUI.
Prompt:
You are a character designer building a reusable SDXL character across renders. Character: [CHARACTER]. THINK SILENTLY: which identity tags MUST stay byte-for-byte fixed (face, hair, eye color, build, signature wardrobe) versus what changes per scene. Then deliver in this exact OUTPUT FORMAT:
Identity block: <one comma-tag line fixing apparent age, face shape, skin tone, eye color, exact hair, body type, signature outfit - ban vague words like 'pretty'; add a unique fictional name token to anchor it>
Scene 1 - [SCENE 1]:
Prompt: <identity block verbatim>, then scene tags + lighting + camera, then quality boosters
Negative prompt: <lowres, deformed hands, extra fingers, bad anatomy, inconsistent face, off-model, text, watermark>
Scene 2 - [SCENE 2]:
Prompt: <identity block verbatim + new scene tags>
Negative prompt: <same core negatives>
Scene 3 - [SCENE 3]:
Prompt: <identity block verbatim + new scene tags>
Negative prompt: <same core negatives>
Consistency note: <one line on locking it further with a LoRA, IP-Adapter, or fixed seed + same Identity block>.
RULE: the Identity block wording must be identical in all three scenes. Nothing else.

Title: Turn Your Selfie Into a Boxed Action Figure: Viral SDXL img2img Recipe
Teaser: Turns one photo into a collectible action-figure-in-blister-pack render with the right img2img tags, denoise, and negatives.
PromptHint: Load your photo as init image; set [YOUR NAME], a [TAGLINE], and [3 ACCESSORIES] that describe you.
Prompt:
You are a toy-product SDXL artist doing the viral action-figure trend via img2img from an uploaded photo. THINK SILENTLY: which tags force a plastic collectible read while preserving the person's face, and what denoise keeps identity. Build ONE comma-tag prompt: photorealistic 3d collectible action figure of a person, recognizable face kept, sealed in retail blister pack, clear plastic bubble on printed cardback, cardback header text '[YOUR NAME]', subtitle '[TAGLINE]', three molded accessory slots holding [3 ACCESSORIES], studio product photography, soft front lighting, slight top-down angle, clean seamless background, then quality boosters (sharp plastic texture:1.2), (accurate finger count:1.1), highly detailed, 8k. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly:
Prompt: <one comma-tag line>
Negative prompt: <warped text, gibberish letters, deformed hands, extra fingers, second figure, melted plastic, blurry, lowres, human skin pores, photorealistic real human, watermark, jpeg artifacts>
Settings (img2img): Denoise 0.45-0.6, Steps 30, CFG 6, Sampler DPM++ 2M Karras, 832x1216; lower denoise = stronger likeness.
Text tip: <one line on rendering the cardback name cleanly, e.g. inpaint the text pass>.
Nothing else.

Title: Photo to Anime Without Losing the Face: SDXL img2img Restyle
Teaser: Restyles a real photo into clean anime via img2img while keeping the person recognizable, with denoise guidance and negatives.
PromptHint: Load the photo as init image; pick an [ANIME STYLE] and the [SETTING] you want around them.
Prompt:
You are an SDXL artist restyling a real photo into anime via img2img on an anime checkpoint. Style: [ANIME STYLE] (e.g. soft Ghibli, 90s cel, modern Shinkai). Setting: [SETTING]. THINK SILENTLY: which photo details preserve likeness (pose, hairstyle, clothing colors, expression) versus what to stylize, and the denoise that keeps identity. Build ONE comma-tag prompt: lead quality tags (masterpiece, best quality), 1person matching the photo, same pose and expression kept, same hairstyle and clothing colors, anime style of [ANIME STYLE], [SETTING] background, hand-painted background, soft volumetric light, clean line art, cel shading, then boosters (detailed eyes:1.1), highly detailed. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly:
Prompt: <one comma-tag line>
Negative prompt: <photorealistic, 3d, realistic skin, extra characters, deformed face, bad hands, extra fingers, off-model, blurry, lowres, watermark, signature, text>
Settings (img2img): Denoise 0.5-0.65, Steps 28, CFG 7, Sampler Euler a, Clip skip 2; lower denoise keeps the face closer.
Likeness tip: <one line on locking identity, e.g. low denoise + IP-Adapter FaceID>.
Nothing else.

Title: Cinematic Cyberpunk Wide Shot: Rain, Neon & Atmosphere in One SDXL Prompt
Teaser: Builds a film-still cyberpunk cityscape tag stack for SDXL - wet streets, volumetric neon, atmosphere - with reflection/color tokens and a noir-tuned negative.
PromptHint: Describe the [SCENE OR CHARACTER]; set a [NEON PALETTE] (e.g. teal+magenta) and an optional [CITY VIBE].
Prompt:
You are an SDXL artist nailing the viral neo-noir / cyberpunk cinematic still. Scene or character: [SCENE OR CHARACTER]. Neon palette: [NEON PALETTE]. City vibe: [CITY VIBE]. THINK SILENTLY: how to read 'noir' (low key, hard rim, wet reflections, atmosphere) without going muddy, where neon should fall, and what gives the frame cinematic depth (foreground occlusion, haze, light bloom). Build ONE comma-tag prompt: subject + framing, wet rain-slicked street with neon reflections in [NEON PALETTE], towering signage and holograms, volumetric fog and steam, [CITY VIBE], hard rim light, cinematic low-key lighting, atmospheric haze, light bloom, shallow depth of field, anamorphic lens flare, then camera tags (35mm, f/2.0, cinematic) and boosters (cinematic:1.15), (dramatic lighting:1.2), ultra detailed, film grain, 8k. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly:
Prompt: <one comma-tag line>
Negative prompt: <flat lighting, daytime, overexposed, washed out, blurry, deformed hands, extra fingers, bad anatomy, plastic skin, cartoon, empty sterile street, text, watermark, jpeg artifacts>
Settings: Steps 32, CFG 5, Sampler DPM++ 2M Karras, 1216x832 (cinematic wide), Hires fix 1.5x.
Mood swap: <one token that turns the frame from menacing to melancholic>.
Nothing else.

Title: Time-Machine Portrait: Send Your Face to Any Decade (SDXL img2img)
Teaser: Generates a film-grain decade portrait (70s/80s/Y2K) tag prompt that keeps the subject recognizable, with era-accurate negatives.
PromptHint: Set the [DECADE] (e.g. 1977, 1985, Y2K) and describe the [SUBJECT]; img2img for likeness.
Prompt:
You are an SDXL artist doing the viral decade time-machine portrait trend. Decade: [DECADE]. Subject: [SUBJECT]. THINK SILENTLY: the era's real film stock, hair, wardrobe, color cast and grain, plus how to keep the face recognizable in img2img. Build ONE comma-tag prompt: subject with same face kept, period-accurate hairstyle and wardrobe for [DECADE], era-correct setting, shot on period film stock (e.g. Kodak Portra for 70s, harsh direct flash for 80s mall, point-and-shoot for Y2K), authentic film grain, light leaks, period color grade, then boosters (analog photo:1.15), realistic, detailed skin texture, 4k. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly:
Prompt: <one comma-tag line>
Negative prompt: <modern clothing, smartphone, digital look, too clean, oversharpened, deformed hands, extra fingers, bad anatomy, plastic skin, text, watermark, anachronism>
Settings (img2img): Denoise 0.5-0.65, Steps 30, CFG 5, Sampler DPM++ 2M Karras; lower denoise keeps likeness.
Era detail: <one period-accurate prop or texture to add for authenticity>.
Nothing else.

Title: Sell-Ready Product Hero Shots: Studio-Lit E-Commerce SDXL Prompt
Teaser: Builds a clean catalog/hero product shot tag stack for SDXL with seamless backdrop, lighting rig, and artifact-killing negatives.
PromptHint: Describe the [PRODUCT]; set a [BACKGROUND] (e.g. seamless white, marble) and optional [MOOD/PALETTE].
Prompt:
You are an SDXL product photographer building a hero shot for e-commerce. Product: [PRODUCT]. Background: [BACKGROUND]. Mood/palette: [MOOD/PALETTE]. THINK SILENTLY: the lighting rig that flatters this material (matte vs glossy vs glass), reflections, and the negatives that kill warped logos and gibberish text. Build ONE comma-tag prompt: product as hero subject, centered, on [BACKGROUND], studio product photography, three-point softbox lighting, soft reflections, gentle shadow, shallow depth of field, [MOOD/PALETTE] color grade, then camera tags (macro lens, f/8, tack sharp) and boosters (product photography:1.2), (high detail:1.15), commercial quality, 8k, clean. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly:
Prompt: <one comma-tag line>
Negative prompt: <blurry, lowres, warped logo, gibberish text, distorted shape, harsh reflections, clutter, dust, fingerprints, deformed, watermark, jpeg artifacts, busy background>
Settings: Steps 30, CFG 5, Sampler DPM++ 2M Karras, 1024x1024, Hires fix.
Layout tip: <one line on using ControlNet/Image Guidance to lock the product silhouette>.
Nothing else.

Title: Design an Original Fantasy Beast: Copyright-Safe SDXL Creature Prompt
Teaser: Designs an original (non-IP) fantasy creature concept prompt with anatomy/biome logic, render style tags, and a clean negative.
PromptHint: Set the [CREATURE CONCEPT] (e.g. glass-winged tundra wolf) and a [BIOME]; optional [RENDER STYLE].
Prompt:
You are a creature concept artist writing SDXL prompts for original, non-trademarked designs. Concept: [CREATURE CONCEPT]. Biome: [BIOME]. Render style: [RENDER STYLE]. THINK SILENTLY: believable anatomy for the biome (camouflage, locomotion, defense), a clear silhouette, and one memorable feature. Build ONE comma-tag prompt: original creature design, [CREATURE CONCEPT], anatomically plausible, distinctive silhouette, biome-adapted features for [BIOME], full-body 3/4 view, neutral concept-art lighting, render style tags ([RENDER STYLE], concept art, creature design, trending on artstation, intricate detail), then boosters (highly detailed:1.2), sharp focus, 8k. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly:
Prompt: <one comma-tag line>
Negative prompt: <deformed anatomy, extra limbs, fused limbs, blurry, low detail, copyrighted character, mascot, cartoon when realism wanted, text, watermark, signature, ugly>
Settings: Steps 35, CFG 6, Sampler DPM++ 3M SDE, 1024x1024, Hires fix 1.5x.
Design note: <one line naming the single feature that makes it memorable>.
Nothing else.

Title: Posters With Text That Actually Reads: SDXL Layout + Inpaint Text Plan
Teaser: Builds a poster/key-art SDXL prompt with composition tags and a realistic plan to fix SDXL's weak text via inpaint or overlay.
PromptHint: Give the [HEADLINE] (exact words in quotes), the [THEME/SCENE], and an optional [PALETTE] in hex.
Prompt:
You are an SDXL art director building a poster, knowing SDXL renders dense text poorly. Headline: "[HEADLINE]". Theme/scene: [THEME/SCENE]. Palette: [PALETTE]. THINK SILENTLY: leave deliberate negative space for the headline, drive composition with tags, and plan the text as a separate pass. Build ONE comma-tag prompt: poster key art, [THEME/SCENE], strong focal subject, deliberate empty space for a headline, dynamic composition, [PALETTE] color scheme, dramatic lighting, then style + boosters (poster art:1.15), cinematic, highly detailed, sharp focus, 4k. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly:
Prompt: <one comma-tag line>
Negative prompt: <gibberish text, misspelled words, warped letters, cluttered, busy, low contrast, blurry, deformed, watermark, jpeg artifacts, no space for text>
Settings: Steps 32, CFG 6, Sampler DPM++ 2M Karras, 832x1216 (portrait poster).
Text plan: 1) generate the art with empty space, 2) inpaint or overlay "[HEADLINE]" in a real font in your editor (SDXL alone garbles long text), 3) optional: route the typography to Ideogram/Nano Banana for accuracy.
Nothing else.

Title: Fix Any Broken SDXL Render: The Negative-Prompt Doctor (Diagnose + Repair)
Teaser: Acts as an SDXL troubleshooter: reads your prompt + what went wrong and returns a rebuilt prompt, a stronger negative, and settings.
PromptHint: Paste your current [PROMPT] and describe [WHAT WENT WRONG] (e.g. melted hands, plastic skin, ignored subject).
Prompt:
You are an SDXL troubleshooting expert (A1111/ComfyUI, Juggernaut & community checkpoints). Current prompt: [PROMPT]. Problem: [WHAT WENT WRONG]. THINK SILENTLY: map each symptom to a real cause - melted hands (anatomy + steps), plastic skin (over-weighting + CFG), ignored subject (token order or over-long prompt), blur (Hires/denoise), washed color (CFG/sampler). Then fix it. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly:
Diagnosis: <2-4 bullets mapping symptom to cause>
Rebuilt prompt: <one comma-tag line, key subject moved earlier, emphasis weights trimmed to only what matters, quality boosters at the end>
Negative prompt: <a targeted negative that directly counters [WHAT WENT WRONG], e.g. deformed hands, extra fingers, mutated hands, plastic skin, oversaturated, plus standard lowres, blurry, bad anatomy, text, watermark>
Settings to change: <Steps, CFG, Sampler, resolution, Hires fix / denoise - state the new value and why>
Quick test: <one A/B change to confirm the fix>.
Nothing else. Note: tag-emphasis and CFG advice is model-version dependent - verify on your checkpoint.

Title: It Interviews You First: The No-Guesswork SDXL Prompt Brief
Teaser: Instead of guessing, the model interviews you to nail subject, style, checkpoint and aspect, then outputs a full SDXL prompt block.
PromptHint: Start with a rough [IMAGE IDEA]; answer the questions; it returns the prompt + negative + settings.
Prompt:
You are an SDXL prompt consultant who refuses to guess. Rough idea: [IMAGE IDEA]. STEP 1 - INTERVIEW: ask me 5-7 diagnostic questions ONE AT A TIME (do not write the prompt yet), and for each propose the answer you'd recommend so I can just say 'yes, go with that'. Cover: realism vs anime vs concept art, checkpoint (Juggernaut XL / Animagine / Pony / other), subject details, mood + lighting, aspect ratio, and whether this is txt2img or img2img from a reference. STEP 2 - after I answer (or say 'use your picks'), produce the final SDXL recipe. OUTPUT FORMAT for the final answer exactly:
Prompt: <one comma-tag line, structured subject-to-boosters, emphasis weights only where needed>
Negative prompt: <a negative tuned to the chosen style and checkpoint>
Settings: <Steps, CFG, Sampler, resolution, Clip skip, Hires/denoise tuned to the checkpoint>
Why these choices: <2-3 bullets>
One variation: <a single token swap to try next>.
Do not output the final block until the interview is done or I say to use your picks. Nothing else.
